---
title: "GPS - Global Patient Safety"
---

Supporting pharmacovigilance, PASS, DSUR/PSUR/PBRER safety reporting, and signal detection analyses.

::: {.callout-warning}
### GPS Schema Restriction
GPS analysts have **read-only access to `rwe_strada_wrk`**. Use `rwe_strada_gps_wrk` as the working schema in all SAS and SQL programs.
:::

### GPS — CLUWE for Safety Data

#### Lilly FTE

Request CLUWE access (Step 2) with the compound safety study folder path from your Lilly Lead. On the CLUWE/RHEL8 SAS profile (port 8564), use `dsn=Access_Redshift` (not `Access_Redshift_Prod`).

#### Vendor / Contractor

Same CLUWE request process as Step 2, but compound safety folder details must come from your Lilly Lead. Safety study folders are per-compound — request access separately for each compound you support. All CLUWE work runs inside Citrix.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### GPS — DSUR / PSUR Statistical Tables

-   Tables **EXPOS1, DEMOG1, DEMOG2** required for DSUR are also required for PSUR. If reporting periods align, the same tables may serve both reports.
-   If DSUR and PSUR are on differing schedules, PSUR deliverables follow the DSUR process — but reports on deaths and discontinuations due to adverse events are **not required for PSUR**.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### GPS — Additional Training

| Course ID | Title |
|-----------|-------|
| 9295008 | LRL: Vault Clinical Foundations |
| 9352650 | LRL: Exploring Data Integrity Fundamentals in Clin Dev & Research |
| 9317635 | MED-QUAL: Evaluation of Potential Data Integrity Issues in Approved Documents |

---

*Content or technical issues with this site? [Click here](maintenance.qmd) to report any issues or provide feedback.*
